## 📦 Skill bundles (dependent suites — promote as a unit)

| Bundle | Repo | Author | Kind | Steps | Why included (reasoning) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| spec-driven-development | `antonarhipov/agentskills` | Anton Arhipov | workflow | 01-spec → 02-criteria → 03-rules → 04-review → 05-tasks → 06-execute | Six-step spec-driven development pipeline (spec → criteria → rules → review → tasks → execute) in the author's dedicated agentskills repo at 13 stars. Members are tightly coupled via explicit file handoffs (spec.md, criteria.md, rules.md, review.md, tasks.yaml) and pipeline-position annotations with arrows. Each step is deeply developed (79–228 lines) with EARS template guidance, B-N/AC/RULE traceability IDs, anti-pattern sections, verification passes, and cross-document conflict detection. This is the canonical, richest version of the author's pipeline; per Rule 5, a coherent, high-quality workflow bundle qualifies as 'found' under the workflow category. _(also copied in: kotlinconf-sdd, midifx, sdd-demo)_ |
| spec-driven-development-workshop | `antonarhipov/sdd-workshop` | Anton Arhipov | workflow | 01-clarify → 02-analyze → 03-constrain → 04-validate → 05-generate-plan | Five-step variant of the same spec-driven development pipeline (clarify → analyze → constrain → validate → generate-plan) living in a workshop/teaching repo at 2 stars. Members are considerably thinner (36–83 lines vs 79–228 in canonical), lacking detailed EARS protocols, traceability IDs, anti-pattern sections, and cross-document conflict logic. Per the Devnexus 2026 single-promotion principle, when a canonical multi-step pipeline (agentskills, 6 richer steps, 13 stars) and a teaching variant of the same pipeline coexist, only the canonical is promoted as 'found'; this workshop variant is 'needs_review' at best. _(also copied in: sdd-workshop)_ |
